details: The objective is to develop these two clusters with world-class infrastructure. The guiding principle behind the design of clusters would be to create world-class infrastructure that caters to the business needs of the local artisans & SMEs to boost production and export. In brief, the main objective of setting up these clusters is to assist the artisans & entrepreneurs in setting up world-class units with modern infrastructure, the latest technology, and adequate training and HRD inputs, coupled with market linkages and production diversification. SPV is designed in such a way, it will have Standard Models of units of SSI and SME with infrastructure that is customized to give a competitive edge and these centers have greater potential to become globally competitive.
benefits: Under the Comprehensive Handicrafts Cluster Development Scheme, interventions such as baseline Survey and activity Mapping, Skill Training, Marketing events, Improved Tool Kits, Publicity, Seminars, capacity building, Design workshops, etc will be provided. The scheme will also provide for Emporiums, Common Facility Centres, Raw Material Banks, Trade Common Production Centres, Facilitation Centres, and Design & Resource Centres.
eligibility: The Implementing Agency (lA), which will be a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV), shall be a legal entity, preferably a company, with the participation of related stakeholders, particularly the leading manufacturers, suppliers, buyers, and artisan federations/SHGs. The SPV shall be selected preferably through an open competitive bidding process. However, selection of SPV would mainly depend on the project profile, activities required to be intervened, and on various other parameters, therefore selection of such suitable SPV after seeking formal approval of PAMC. As the Handicrafts sector is highly unorganized, the procedure for selection of SPV in special cases shall be kept open for PAMC/Government.

offline: Application to be submitted by the Executing Agency (EA) at the concerned Regional office of the Textile Commissioner.

documents_required: 3.Sale /lease deed of land/building in the name of SPV.

faqs.question: What is the Comprehensive Handicrafts Cluster Development Programme (CHCDP)?
faqs.answer: The CHCDP is a scheme by the Ministry of Textiles designed to develop handicrafts clusters with world-class infrastructure , aiming to enhance production, export, skill training, and business competitiveness for artisans and SMEs. Ministry of Textiles handicrafts clusters world-class infrastructure

faqs.question: What is the main objective of CHCDP?
faqs.answer: The objective is to assist artisans and entrepreneurs in: Setting up modern, well-equipped units Accessing cutting-edge technology Receiving training and HR development Getting market linkages and support for production diversification The objective is to assist artisans and entrepreneurs in: Setting up modern, well-equipped units Accessing cutting-edge technology Receiving training and HR development Getting market linkages and support for production diversification

faqs.question: Who can apply under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Only a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) can apply. The SPV must be a legal entity (preferably a company) comprising stakeholders like: Leading manufacturers Suppliers Buyers Artisan federations or Self-Help Groups (SHGs) Only a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) can apply. The SPV must be a legal entity (preferably a company) comprising stakeholders like: Leading manufacturers Suppliers Buyers Artisan federations or Self-Help Groups (SHGs)

faqs.question: How is the SPV selected?
faqs.answer: Generally, the SPV is selected through an open competitive bidding process. However, due to the unorganized nature of the handicrafts sector, the Project Approval and Monitoring Committee (PAMC) or the Government may select SPVs directly in special cases.

faqs.question: What are the key interventions or benefits offered under the CHCDP?
faqs.answer: The scheme supports: Baseline survey & activity mapping Skill training & capacity building Improved toolkits Marketing events, seminars, and design workshops Establishment of Common Facility Centres, Raw Material Banks, Emporiums, and Design & Resource Centres Baseline survey & activity mapping Skill training & capacity building Improved toolkits Marketing events, seminars, and design workshops Common Facility Centres, Raw Material Banks, Emporiums, Design & Resource Centres

faqs.question: Is this scheme for individual artisans?
faqs.answer: No , this is a cluster-level infrastructure development scheme. However, individual artisans benefit indirectly through enhanced production facilities, skill training, market access, and better working environments. cluster-level infrastructure development scheme. individual artisans

faqs.question: What infrastructure will be created under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme will help develop: Trade Common Production Centres Design & Resource Centres Facilitation Centres Emporiums and Common Facility Centres (CFCs) for artisans’ use Trade Common Production Centres Trade Common Production Centres Design & Resource Centres Design & Resource Centres Facilitation Centres Facilitation Centres Emporiums and Common Facility Centres (CFCs) for artisans’ use Emporiums and Common Facility Centres (CFCs)

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply for CHCDP?
faqs.answer: The Executing Agency (EA) must submit: Detailed Project Report (DPR) Nature of SPV Sale/Lease Deed of land/building in the name of SPV Non-agricultural land certificate Approved layout plan Detailed Project Report (DPR) Detailed Project Report (DPR) Nature of SPV Nature of SPV Sale/Lease Deed of land/building in the name of SPV Sale/Lease Deed Non-agricultural land certificate Non-agricultural land certificate Approved layout plan Approved layout plan

faqs.question: What is the application process for the scheme?
faqs.answer: The process is offline . The Executing Agency (EA) must submit the application and necessary documents at the concerned Regional Office of the Textile Commissioner. offline Executing Agency (EA) concerned Regional Office of the Textile Commissioner.

faqs.question: Who implements the CHCDP scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is implemented through a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) selected by the Project Approval and Monitoring Committee (PAMC) under the Office of the Development Commissioner (Handicrafts) , Ministry of Textiles. Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) Project Approval and Monitoring Committee (PAMC) Office of the Development Commissioner (Handicrafts)
